textPlain: Lots of good content here, but it's also heavy content marketing for ClickHouse. Completely fine with people showcasing why their tool is better, but I'm not looking for a solution this morning, just curious about general architecture learnings. Asked Claude to separate the advertisements from the architecture principles, here's the summary if anyone else is curious:---Core Architecture Design Principles1. Shifting Left in Data PipelinesThe principle of "shifting left" emphasizes structuring and optimizing data as early as possible in the pipeline. By properly typing, deduplicating, and structuring data at the source, you avoid expensive transformations downstream and reduce data quality issues throughout the analytics stack.2. Freshness vs. Accuracy TradeoffReal-time analytics inherently involves balancing data freshness with accuracy. The moment data is loaded, it begins to become outdated, so systems must ensure consistency across related tables when refreshing to maintain cohesive and accurate results.3. Minimize Query-Time ComplexityMove computational complexity from query time to either insert time or pre-processing phases:Denormalization: Flatten related tables into single structures, trading storage efficiency for dramatic query performance gainsPre-aggregation: Compute aggregates as data arrives rather than when users request it4. Multi-Level Optimization StrategyImplement optimization at multiple levels simultaneously:- Query Level: Physical data sorting based on common access patterns- Storage Level: Partitioning strategies that enable data skipping for irrelevant segments- Processing Level: Incremental processing that only updates changed data segments5. Columnar Storage BenefitsStore data in column-oriented format where all values belonging to a single column are stored together. This enables fast analytical queries on specific columns from potentially hundreds of available columns by drastically reducing disk I/O.6. Partitioning StrategyDesign partitio
